data.title: Potential Dollar Decline Amid Iran Conflict and Trump Administration Risks
data.description:
Analysts from UniCredit Bank, led by Roberto Mialich, suggest that the U.S. dollar could face further declines if the conflict in Iran concludes or becomes a risk factor for the Trump administration. According to Odaily, Mialich highlights that the dollar's role as a safe haven could diminish if risk appetite improves, as observed after U.S. President Donald Trump announced a two-week ceasefire.

Mialich notes that once the conflict ends, markets may refocus on factors that previously pressured the dollar before the war. Additionally, if the U.S. becomes embroiled in a prolonged conflict without a clear exit strategy, the dollar might weaken further. This situation could lead to renewed concerns about U.S. economic policy and the credibility of the dollar.

data.title: Thai SEC Proposes Regulatory Oversight for Crypto Enterprise Funders
data.description:
The Thai Securities and Exchange Commission (Thai SEC) has proposed extending regulatory oversight to include the funders behind major shareholders of cryptocurrency enterprises. According to ChainCatcher, this proposal aims to ensure that both direct and indirect supporters of these enterprises undergo the same level of regulatory scrutiny as shareholders.

The initiative seeks to curb hidden financial flows and prevent illegal funds from infiltrating crypto businesses. The rules would also apply to those providing financial support through equity acquisitions. The Thai SEC stated that 'significant financial support' includes guarantees, contractual arrangements, or any investment tools that effectively position the funder as an investor. If the primary shareholder is a government-related entity, only the entity-level shareholding will be reviewed.

data.title: World Liberty Financial Launches Ethereum-Based Lending Market
data.description: World Liberty Financial has introduced a new lending market within the Ethereum ecosystem by integrating with the decentralized lending protocol Dolomite. According to ChainCatcher, the current yield for USD1 stands at 34.51%. It is important to note that interest rates are subject to change, and the most accurate rates can be found on the product's website.

data.title: Standard Chartered Considers Internal Management of Zodia Custody
data.description: Standard Chartered is contemplating bringing certain operations of its digital asset storage company, Zodia Custody, under internal management. According to BlockBeats, the bank plans to merge Zodia's crypto custody services with its Luxembourg digital asset division. Meanwhile, Zodia will continue to operate independently as a software-as-a-service business.

data.title: Trump's TACO Move Boosts Markets Amid US-Iran Ceasefire Uncertainty
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's recent TACO initiative has led to a surge in market activity, Bloomberg posted on X. However, the newly established two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran continues to leave significant issues unaddressed. The ceasefire aims to reduce tensions but does not resolve underlying conflicts between the two nations. Analysts suggest that while the market response has been positive, the long-term implications of the ceasefire remain uncertain. The agreement is seen as a temporary measure, with experts calling for more comprehensive negotiations to address the broader geopolitical challenges. The situation continues to evolve, with stakeholders closely monitoring developments.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Announces Immediate Tariffs on Goods from Countries Supplying Arms to Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced on social media that any country providing military weapons to Iran will face an immediate 50% tariff on all goods exported to the United States. According to Odaily, this measure will be implemented without any exceptions or exemptions.

data.title: TRON Ecosystem March Review Highlights Community Achievements
data.description:
The TRON ecosystem's March review has been officially released, showcasing significant community-driven achievements. According to ChainCatcher, the atmosphere of collaborative development within the ecosystem continues to intensify. TRON founder Justin Sun publicly recognized several outstanding creators for their contributions.

Creators who successfully reached their March milestones will receive a 'Sunshine Reward' of 100 USDT, distributed in $TRX. The TRON ECO plans to further enhance its reward mechanism, expand the prize pool, and continue empowering quality creators and contributors. This initiative aims to work alongside global community members to boost the influence of the TRON ecosystem.

data.title: UniCredit Bank: If the war with Iran ends, the US dollar may fall further.
data.description: UniCredit analyst Roberto Mialich stated in a report that the dollar could fall further if the war with Iran ends or becomes a risk factor for the Trump administration. He pointed out that if risk appetite eases, the dollar's safe-haven role means it will lose support, as seen after Trump announced a two-week ceasefire. He stated, "Once the conflict finally ends, the market will refocus on the factors that pressured the dollar before the war began." The dollar could also weaken if the US becomes embroiled in a protracted conflict without a clear exit strategy. He indicated that this could trigger a new round of concerns about US economic policy and the dollar's credibility. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. President Signs GENIUS Act to Regulate Stablecoin Reserves
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has signed the GENIUS Act, mandating stablecoin issuers to back their coins with high-quality assets at a minimum 1:1 ratio, such as U.S. dollars, short-term Treasury bonds, reverse repos, and money market funds. According to PANews, the act also prohibits direct interest payments to stablecoin holders.

The White House Council of Economic Advisers' model indicates that under baseline conditions, the prohibition on stablecoin yields could increase bank loans by approximately $2.1 billion, representing only 0.02% of total loans, with a net welfare cost of around $800 million. Large banks are expected to contribute about 76% of the new loans, while community banks would account for approximately 24%.

Even under extreme assumptions, such as reserves being entirely non-lendable cash and the Federal Reserve abandoning its current framework, the increase in bank loans would only be about 4.4%. The report suggests that the yield ban offers minimal protection for bank lending but could weaken the competitive benefits brought by stablecoins.

data.title: White House spokesperson: Discussions regarding face-to-face talks between the US and Iran are currently underway.
data.description: White House spokesperson: Discussions regarding face-to-face talks between the US and Iran are ongoing, but everything will be confirmed only after a formal announcement by the president or the White House. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Following the release of CZ's new book, Binance Life rose by over 0.09 USDT.
data.description: Market data shows that Binance Life has risen above 0.09 USDT, currently trading at 0.0965 USDT, a 24-hour increase of 35.65%.

data.title: Aave V4 Deposits Surpass $10 Million in Security-Focused Growth Phase
data.description: Aave has announced that deposits in its V4 platform have exceeded $10 million during its security-focused growth phase. According to Foresight News, as the system continues to mature, the limits on supply and borrowing will gradually increase.

data.title: STOCKS | A-Share Market Rebounds Amid Temporary US-Iran Ceasefire
data.description:
On April 8, Jin10 reported that the A-share market experienced a significant rebound following news of a temporary ceasefire between the United States and Iran. According to Jin10, the CSI 300 Index, representing blue-chip stocks, showed stronger performance compared to the broader Shanghai Composite Index, indicating a faster recovery pace for high-quality stocks. The valuation recovery space and speed for these stocks are expected to surpass the overall market, gradually widening the gap between high-quality and underperforming stocks.

The A-share market's movements are closely linked to external factors, capital flows, and the fundamentals of listed companies. The temporary easing of geopolitical tensions has removed a major uncertainty, providing an opportunity for valuation recovery in the previously risk-averse market. When selecting investment directions, market funds tend to prioritize low-risk and highly certain listed companies. High-quality stocks, with stable operating performance, solid fundamentals, and predictable cash dividend capabilities, have become the preferred targets for large funds during the rebound phase. This preference is a key reason for their leading stock price performance.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Launches 100th Wave of Operation True Promise 4
data.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced that its Aerospace Force has initiated the 100th wave of Operation True Promise 4, targeting 25 sites. According to NS3.AI, the operation included strikes on 13 energy complexes and oil pipelines associated with the United States and Israel. Additionally, Iran reported that the strikes impacted 10 military, security, and logistical locations, as well as technical and infrastructure targets along enemy movement routes.

data.title: Iran claims to have launched 100 waves of strikes.
data.description: The Public Relations Department of the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps issued a statement today stating that in the 100th wave of Operation "True Commitment 4," the Aerospace Forces launched a fierce attack on 25 strategic targets, including 13 energy complexes and oil pipelines related to the United States and Israel, 10 military, security, and logistical targets, and several technical and infrastructure targets along the enemy's route of advance, inflicting heavy losses on the enemy. (CCTV International News)

data.title: Exxon Mobil Reports 6% Drop in Global Production Due to US-Israel Conflict Impacting Persian Gulf
data.description: Exxon Mobil has announced that 6% of its global production in the first quarter was disrupted due to the ongoing conflict involving the U.S. and Israel against Iran, which has significantly affected the Persian Gulf's energy sector. Bloomberg posted on X that the geopolitical tensions have led to substantial operational challenges for energy companies in the region. The conflict has caused disruptions in the supply chain and production processes, impacting the overall output of major oil producers. Exxon Mobil's announcement highlights the broader implications of geopolitical conflicts on global energy markets, as companies navigate the complexities of operating in conflict zones. The situation underscores the vulnerability of the energy sector to geopolitical instability, with potential long-term effects on global oil supply and prices.

data.title: Gate expands its local payment channels in Mexico and continues to improve its global fiat currency deposit network.
data.description: According to official sources, cryptocurrency exchange Gate recently launched a localized cryptocurrency purchase channel in Mexico, allowing users to efficiently purchase cryptocurrencies via local real-time bank transfers and bank cards. By integrating with the banking system and telecom operator payment networks, this significantly improves the convenience and accessibility of deposits for users in emerging markets. In terms of regional expansion, the newly launched SPEI local bank real-time transfer system in Mexico further connects payment channels in regions such as Brazil, continuously improving Gate's local payment network in Latin America. With the continued growth in demand for digital assets in Latin America, localized, low-cost fiat currency deposit channels are becoming a key entry point. Gate will continue to leverage local financial infrastructure to advance its localization strategy, further bridging the gap between traditional payment methods and the cryptocurrency ecosystem.

data.title: Meme Coin GIGGLE Experiences Short-Term Surge on BSC Chain
data.description:
On April 8, the meme coin GIGGLE saw a brief increase in market value on the BSC chain, according to BlockBeats On-chain Detection. The coin's value surged over 10%, reaching $28.4 million before settling back to $27.5 million, marking a 24-hour increase of 12%. 

BlockBeats notes that meme coins often experience significant trading volatility, largely driven by market sentiment and speculative concepts, without any inherent value or practical use. Investors are advised to be cautious of the risks involved.

data.title: Quantum Computers: Catalyst for Technological Evolution, Not a Threat to Bitcoin
data.description: Quantum computers are not considered an existential threat to Bitcoin, according to Bernstein. Instead, they are viewed as a catalyst for technological evolution. According to NS3.AI, the risk posed by quantum computing is not new and extends beyond cryptocurrencies. Developers of Bitcoin and Ethereum are actively working on quantum resistance through initiatives like the BIP360 proposal and Ethereum upgrade roadmaps.

data.title: Amundi Capitalizes on Equity Market Selloff Amid Iran Conflict Concerns
data.description: Amundi, a leading asset management firm, has strategically increased its investments in equity markets during a period of widespread selloff. Bloomberg posted on X that this move comes as many investors have been reducing their stock holdings due to heightened fears of an escalation in the conflict involving Iran. Amundi's decision to buy into the market downturn has proven to be beneficial, as the firm capitalized on the situation while others were retreating. This approach highlights Amundi's confidence in the market's resilience despite geopolitical tensions.
